chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الْمُتَمَتِّعِ تَعْرِضُ لَهُ الْحَاجَةُ خَارِجاً مِنْ مَكَّةَ بَعْدَ إِحْلَالِهِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 148 – A need presents itself to the performer of Tumatto, outside of Makkah, after his being free from Ihraam

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/87)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/87)
text: رَجُلٍ قَضَى مُتْعَتَهُ ثُمَّ عَرَضَتْ لَهُ حَاجَةٌ أَرَادَ أَنْ يَخْرُجَ إِلَيْهَا قَالَ فَقَالَ فَلْيَغْتَسِلْ لِلْإِحْرَامِ وَ لْيُهِلَّ بِالْحَجِّ وَ لْيَمْضِ فِي حَاجَتِهِ وَ إِنْ لَمْ يَقْدِرْ عَلَى الرُّجُوعِ إِلَى مَكَّةَ مَضَى إِلَى عَرَفَاتٍ .

en.hubeali: Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Hafs Bin Al Bakhtary,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws regarding a man who fulfilled his Tumatto, then a need presented itself to him, so he intended to go out to it’. So heasws said: ‘So let him what for the Ihraam, and let him begin with the Hajj, and let him go regarding his need; and if he is not able upon the return to Makkah, he should go to Arafaat’.
en.sarwar: 4. Ibn abu ‘Umayr has narrated from Hafs ibn al-Bakhtariy who has said the following: “About a man who completes Mut‘ah then because of a certain work wants to go out, he (the Imam) said, ‘He must take Ghusl (bath) for Ihram and say Talbiyah for al-Hajj, then go for his work and if he cannot return to Makkah he must go straight to ‘Arafat.’”
